description.para: These constants specify the handler events that an application can handle using its specific handler function.
Note that these constants are not bit mask, so only one event can be passed to an application at a time.

briefdescription.para: Alarm generated event.
A new second generated event.
< prevent circular inclusions
Separate Mask for Crystal oscillator bit Enable.
Unix epoch year (1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC)
Weekday of epoch (0=Sun, 1=Mon, 2=Tue, 3=Wed, 4=Thu, 5=Fri, 6=Sat)
Check if a year is a leap year using complete Gregorian calendar rules:

listitem.para: Exception: Divisible by 100: not a leap year
Exception to exception: Divisible by 400: leap year Examples: 2000 (leap), 2004 (leap), 2100 (not leap)

briefdescription.para: Calibration value.
Oscillator frequency.
This macro updates the current time of RTC device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.
is the desired time for RTC in seconds.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XRtcPsu_SetTime(XRtcPsu *InstancePtr, u32 Time)

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the last set time of RTC device.

detaileddescription.para: Whenever a reset happens, the last set time will be zeroth day first sec.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.

simplesect.para: The last set time in seconds.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the calibration value of RTC device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the current time of RTC device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.

simplesect.para: Current Time. This current time will be in seconds.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the control register value of RTC device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.
is the desired control register value for RTC.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XRtcPsu_SetControlRegister(XRtcPsu *InstancePtr, u32 Value)

briefdescription.para: This macro returns the safety check register value of RTC device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro sets the safety check register value of RTC device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.
is a safety check value to be written in register.

simplesect.para: C-Style signature: void XRtcPsu_SetSafetyCheck(XRtcPsu *InstancePtr, u32 Value)

briefdescription.para: This macro resets the alarm register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This macro rounds off the given number.

parameterdescription.para: is the one that needs to be rounded off..

simplesect.para: The rounded off value of the input number.

briefdescription.para: This data type defines a handler that an application defines to communicate with interrupt system to retrieve state information about an application.

parameterdescription.para: is a callback reference passed in by the upper layer when setting the handler, and is passed back to the upper layer when the handler is called. It is used to find the device driver instance.
contains one of the event constants indicating events that have occurred.

briefdescription.para: This table contains configuration information for RTC device in the system.
This function initializes a XRtcPsu instance/driver.

detaileddescription.para:
The initialization entails:
Initialize all members of the XRtcPsu structure.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.
points to the XRtcPsu device configuration structure.

is the device base address in the virtual memory address space. If the address translation is not used then the physical address is passed. Unexpected errors may occur if the address mapping is changed after this function is invoked.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the alarm value of RTC device.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance
is the desired alarm time for RTC.
says whether the alarm need to set at periodic Intervals or a one-time alarm.

briefdescription.para: This function translates time in seconds to a YEAR:MON:DAY HR:MIN:SEC format and saves it in the DT structure variable.

detaileddescription.para:
Seconds
is the time value that has to be shown in DateTime format.
dt
is the DateTime format variable that stores the translated time.
None.
This API uses Unix epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC) as reference. Supports years from 1970 to 2106 (unsigned 32-bit limit). WEEKDAY CONVENTION: WeekDay field follows POSIX standard: 0=Sunday, 1=Monday, 2=Tuesday, 3=Wednesday, 4=Thursday, 5=Friday, 6=Saturday 1970-01-01 (epoch) was Thursday, so WeekDay = 4.
For u32 input, overflow is impossible. Maximum timestamp (0xFFFFFFFF) correctly converts to 2106-02-07 06:28:15.

briefdescription.para: This function translates time in YEAR:MON:DAY HR:MIN:SEC format to seconds since Unix epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC).

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to a DateTime format structure variable of time that has to be converted to seconds.

simplesect.para: This API uses Unix epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC) as reference. Input must be in range 1970-2106. The input structure is not modified. This is the inverse of XRtcPsu_SecToDateTime().

briefdescription.para: This function calculates the calibration value depending on the actual realworld time and also helps in deriving new calibration value if the user wishes to change his oscillator frequency.

detaileddescription.para: TimeReal should be the accurate reference time in seconds since Unix epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC). This matches standard internet time sources (NTP, GPS, etc.) and can be used directly without conversion.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.

is the actual realworld time in seconds since Unix epoch (1970-01-01). This can be obtained from network time protocols (NTP) or other accurate time sources. The value must be greater than the RTC's current time for calibration to work correctly.
is the Oscillator new frequency. If using the typical 32768Hz crystal, then input the same frequency value.

simplesect.para: After calculating the calibration register value, user / application must call CfgInitialize API again to apply the new calibration into effect.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the seconds event status by reading interrupt status register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.

simplesect.para: Returns 1 if a new second event is generated.Else 0..
This API is used in polled mode operation of RTC. This also clears interrupt status seconds bit.

briefdescription.para: This function returns the alarm event status by reading interrupt status register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.

simplesect.para: Returns 1 if the alarm event is generated.Else 0.
This API is used in polled mode operation of RTC. This also clears interrupt status alarm bit.

briefdescription.para: This function gets the current RTC time.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the RTC time by writing into rtc write register.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance.
that should be updated into RTC write register.

briefdescription.para: This function sets the interrupt mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance
contains the interrupts to be enabled. A '1' enables an interrupt, and a '0' disables.

briefdescription.para: This function clears the interrupt mask.

parameterdescription.para: is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance
contains the interrupts to be disabled. A '1' enables an interrupt, and a '0' disables.

briefdescription.para: This function is the interrupt handler for the driver.

detaileddescription.para: It must be connected to an interrupt system by the application such that it can be called when an interrupt occurs.

parameterdescription.para: contains a pointer to the driver instance

briefdescription.para: This function sets the handler that will be called when an event (interrupt) occurs that needs application's attention.

detaileddescription.para:
InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance
FunctionPtr
is the pointer to the callback function.
CallBackRef
is the upper layer callback reference passed back when the callback function is invoked.
None.
There is no assert on the CallBackRef since the driver doesn't know what it is (nor should it)

briefdescription.para: This function runs a self-test on the driver and hardware device.

detaileddescription.para: This self test writes reset value into safety check register and read backs the same. If mismatch offers, returns the failure.

InstancePtr
is a pointer to the XRtcPsu instance
XST_SUCCESS if the test was successful
This function can hang if the hardware is not functioning properly.

briefdescription.para: Looks up the device configuration based on the unique device ID/BaseAddress.

detaileddescription.para: The XRtcPsu_ConfigTable[] contains the configuration info for each device in the system.

parameterdescription.para: contains the base address of the device

simplesect.para: A pointer to the configuration found or NULL if the specified device ID/BaseAddress was not found. See xrtcpsu.h for the definition of XRtcPsu_Config.
In XSCT/classic flow, DeviceId is used to look up the device configuration.
